description: Full system administrator. Can manage users, roles, and view all audit logs. Granted only to platform operators; changes are logged to the immutable audit trail and reviewed weekly by compliance.
Team manager with read access to team data, report viewing, and request approval. Cannot see cross-team data or modify user records. Sees redacted PII in all reports.
Software engineer with code repository and staging deployment access. Cannot deploy to production directly — production pushes require a devops role with a two-person approval.
Support agent who can assist users by resetting passwords and viewing tickets. Cannot suspend, reassign roles, or view audit logs. All password resets are logged and emailed to the user.
Read-only compliance auditor with audit log and reporting access. Exports are watermarked with the auditor's user_id and are disabled for 24 hours after any audit_logs schema change.
Limited intern access. Public documentation only. Cannot view user data, logs, jobs, or configuration. Access auto-expires 90 days after onboarding unless renewed by a manager.

DevOps engineer responsible for production deployments and infrastructure. Required for restart_job, cache invalidation, and rotate_secrets actions. Production deploys require a second devops approval via the two-person rule.
Security administrator who reviews access anomalies, suspends compromised accounts, and maintains RBAC policy definitions. All suspend/unsuspend actions are logged with a justification field.

Data analyst with read access to the analytics warehouse and reporting tools. Queries are rate-limited to protect the primary replica. Cannot view raw PII; all analyst queries receive redacted output.
External stakeholder with view-only access to public dashboards. Cannot see internal logs, user data, or reports. Useful for partner demos and investor updates.
